title: Senior Operations Analyst – Data Integration
description:
As the Senior Operations Analyst – Data Integration you will work within Reedy Creek Energy Services (RCES) organization. RCES Cast Members work on behalf of the Central Florida Oversight District (CFTOD) utility systems across the Walt Disney World Resort. In this role, you will lead data analytics for RCES Program Development, RCES Operational Services \& Strategy, and RCES Senior Leadership for decision making and provide helpful analytics in response to open\-ended program requests. You will also validate data for external reporting to essential partners including Facilities Operations Services (FOS), Industrial Engineering, Finance, Operations, and more. As a storyteller of data, you will transform complex information into compelling narratives to assist with driving organizational initiatives.


This is a full\-time on\-site opportunity based in Lake Buena Vista, FL.

**Responsibilities/You Will**
-----------------------------

* Create and maintain automated reports that support business needs and leadership decisions.
* Build dashboards that show key metrics, trends, and organizational performance.
* Evaluate, transform, and analyze data to support projects, document processes, and present findings.
* Work across teams to ensure accurate data entry, validate information, track new projects, and provide ongoing analysis.
* Translate complex data into clear, meaningful insights that explain the story behind the numbers.

**Basic Qualifications/You Will Have**
--------------------------------------

* 3\+ years of experience in data analysis, working with large and complex datasets.
* Ability to read and write SQL queries to retrieve, analyze, and validate data across large databases.
* Experience in IBM Maximo, SAP, and Microsoft 365 platforms, including navigating the systems, pulling operational data, and translating insights into actionable decisions.
* Experience with Power BI or similar BI tools, including creating dashboards, building data models, and connecting to data sources such as IBM Maximo, SAP, and Microsoft 365 platforms.
* Experience with Smartsheet platform, including developing automated workflows, reports, and other process‑driven solutions.
* Strong attention to data accuracy with the curiosity to investigate anomalies and explain data‑driven findings.
* Ability to combine data from multiple sources into clear insights and recommendations for leadership.
* Ability to turn data requests into actionable analyses and clearly communicate results to partners at all levels.
* Proven ability to build and maintain productive relationships across the organization.
* Effective time‑management skills, with the ability to balance multiple high‑priority tasks and meet deadlines.
* Advanced Excel skills, including complex formulas, data manipulation, and analytical functions.
* Strong understanding of business processes.

**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

* Experience using Python or similar analytical tools to integrate and automate data flows between Smartsheet and other systems through Application Programming Interfaces (APIs).
* Experience with Tableau or comparable data visualization platforms for creating interactive dashboards, performing data analysis, and integrating with IBM Maximo, SAP and Microsoft 365 platforms.

**Education**
-------------

* Bachelor’s degree in data science, statistics, computer science, information systems, finance, business administration, or a related field; or an equivalent combination of education and practical experience working with analytical tools, data integrations (including APIs), and business intelligence platforms such as Python, Power BI, Tableau, or Smartsheet is required.

**Additional Information**
--------------------------


Benefits and Perks: Disney offers a rewards package to help you live your best life. This includes health and savings benefits, educational opportunities and special extras that only Disney can provide. Learn more abo

title: Club Compliance Data Analyst II
description:
**We Put the World on Vacation**


Travel \+ Leisure Co. is the world’s leading vacation ownership and travel membership company, with a dynamic and growing portfolio of resort, travel club, and lifestyle travel brands. Our dedicated associates help the company achieve its mission to put the world on vacation. Innovation and growth keep our work interesting and fun. Every day is a chance to learn something new and turn vacation inspiration into exceptional experiences for millions of travelers worldwide.  

The **Club Compliance Data Analyst II** will lead internal investigations to conduct a thorough analysis of Club compliance. This position plays a key role in identifying, investigating, and evaluating violations of the Club rules. Additionally, this role has the responsibility to propose additional regulation/compliance measures once areas of improvement are identified to meet company compliance objectives, makes key decisions regarding the design and approach of business solutions with minimal supervision and makes key decisions regarding the design and approach of business solutions with minimal supervision.


This position designs and analyzes portfolio reporting on operational and financial trends using a variety of techniques, ranging from simple data aggregation via statistical analysis to data mining. Assists with the design, development, testing, and maintenance of key operational databases, macro programs, and data queries. It also ensures security, stability, and data integrity of internal systems, fulfills standard and ad\-hoc reporting needs and maintains the accuracy and integrity of report data and works with other functional areas within the company to establish performance and operational metrics to improve and measure capability across the organization.


**How You'll Shine:**

**Analysis and Investigation (40%)**

* Analyze booking trends and benefit usage across accounts to identify trends, unapproved collaborations, or rule violations.
* Collaborate with business unit leaders and specialized departments such as Legal, Club, Operations, Title Services, and HR to determine thresholds and successfully investigate all Club, Legal, or Compliance potential violations.
* Lead process evaluations and identify opportunities to improve efficiencies.
* Follow up on outstanding action points within an agreed\-upon timetable and ensure all issues are closed in a timely manner.

**Evaluation, Monitoring, Controlling (10%)**

* Monitor all issues identified during the analysis and investigation process.
* Develop controls to mitigate future risk.
* Collaborate with Legal, Club and HR to evaluate controls related to the following areas of focus: Point Utilization, Booking Trends, Contact Center Operations, Sales and Marketing, and 3rd Party Activity.
* Formulate recommendations to improve internal control processes and other value\-added opportunities, as appropriate.

**Data Reporting, Manipulation, Analysis and Story Telling (40%)**

* Creating reports and developing tools and processes to automate reports.
* Analyzing large amounts of data relating to sales, customers, or financial performance.
* Using Structured Query Language (SQL) and other coding languages to extract data.
* Aligning reports to business objectives and key performance indicators.
* Collaborating with other business areas and leaders to identify reporting needs.
* Accessing existing databases and creating processes for storage of new data.
* Planning \& Forecasting: Identify operational or financial variances and develop and implement forecast models for planning and control.

**Perform other duties as needed (10%)**

**Travel Requirements**

*NONE*

**What You'll Bring:**

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree in MIS, IS, IT or Business, or equivalent work experience\-One to three years of experience in the following:
* Contact Center/Customer Operations
* Designing automation and data connections using any of the following...
* Power Apps, SharePoi

title: Consumer Business Intelligence Developer
description:
The SouthState story is one of steady growth, deep community roots, and an unwavering commitment to helping our customers move forward. Since our beginnings in the 1930s to becoming a trusted financial partner across the South and beyond \- we are known for combining personal relationships with forward\-thinking solutions.
  
  

We are committed to helping our team members find their success while maintaining the integrity of our values: building trust, fostering lasting relationships and pursuing excellence. At SouthState, individual contributions are recognized, potential is cultivated and team members are inspired to achieve their greater purpose. Your future begins here!
  
  

Summary
  
  

A Business Intelligence Developer serves as a strategic driver of data\-driven decision making, architecting and sustaining enterprise\-grade data solutions that underpin financial reporting, risk management, regulatory compliance, and long\-term business strategy. This role synthesizes large\-scale financial and customer datasets into high\-impact, actionable intelligence through sophisticated dashboards, reporting frameworks, and advanced analytics. Additionally, the position plays a critical stewardship function by enforcing data integrity, optimizing performance, and ensuring the scalability, reliability, and resilience of the organization’s data ecosystem.
  
  

Duties \& Responsibilities
  
  

Design and deliver enterprise\-grade Power BI dashboards that create a single source of truth, provide actionable insights, and support strategic decisions. Develop and maintain datasets through efficient data modeling, DAX, and performance\-focused measures, while optimizing SQL queries, views, improve reporting, analytics, and data processing performance.
  
  

Monitor, troubleshoot, and continuously improve production reporting and analytics solutions by resolving issues, ensuring reliability and data accuracy, and optimizing dataset performance, scalability, and overall BI environment efficiency
  
  

Coordinate and collaborate with IT and Line of Business leadership to gather requirements and translate business needs into scalable, data\-driven business intelligence solutions, including data availability, data mapping, integration of new data sources, and governance and security across all reporting and analytics deliverables.
  
  

Maintains compliance with banking regulations (e.g., data governance, audit requirements)
  
  

Perform additional duties and responsibilities as assigned by management to support evolving business needs and organizational priorities.
  
  

It is the responsibility of this role to take ownership of all tasks and challenges that they encounter in the operation of their assigned position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  
  

Qualifications
  
  

Education Requirements
  
  

Bachelor’s degree in quantitative or technical field, or equivalent practical experience
  
  

Minimum Experience
  
  

6\+ years in Business Intelligence, with at least 4\+ years specifically dedicated to the PowerBI ecosystem in enterprise environment
  
  

Banking experience is preferred
  
  

Licenses \& Certifications
  
  

Refer to the Knowledge, Skills and Abilities section below.
  
  

Additional Physical Demands
  
  

Work is primarily performed in an office or remote environment and requires extended periods of computer use, including working with data, dashboards, and reporting tools, with the ability to maintain focus and accuracy while managing multiple analytical tasks.
  
  

Knowledge, Skills, \& Abilities
  
  

**Technical Mastery:** Demonstrates expert\-level proficiency in Power BI, including advanced DAX, Power Query, and the design of complex star schema data models.
  
  

**BI Theory:** Possesses a strong understanding of data warehousing principles and modern business intelligence best practices.

title: Director, Business Intelligence & Analytics - 992052
description:
**We are excited that you are considering joining Nova Southeastern University!**



Nova Southeastern University (NSU) was founded in 1964, and is a not\-for\-profit, independent university with a reputation for academic excellence and innovation. Nova Southeastern University offers competitive salaries, a comprehensive benefits package including tuition waiver, retirement plan, excellent medical and dental plans and much more. NSU cares about the health and welfare of its students, faculty, staff, and campus visitors and is a tobacco\-free university.



We appreciate your support in making NSU the preeminent place to live, work, study and grow. Thank you for your interest in a career with Nova Southeastern University.

  


**Primary Purpose:**



Contributes to the University’s success by leading the strategic transformation, governance alignment, and operational delivery of enterprise business intelligence capabilities. Responsible for modernizing the Business Intelligence (BI) function through adoption of Microsoft Fabric and best\-in\-class analytics practices, advancing trusted data products, and enabling decision\-ready insights. Partners closely with Data Governance, Data Engineering, and institutional stakeholders to ensure the BI team serves as a consultative, trusted advisor model that enhances decision\-making, data literacy, and institutional performance.

  


**Job Category:** Exempt


**Hiring Range:** Commensurate with experience


**Pay Basis:** Annually


**Subject to Grant Funding?** No


**Essential Job Functions:**



1\. Leads and supports the strategic vision, roadmap, and execution for enterprise BI, aligning with institutional data strategy and governance priorities.  

2\. Directs the transition of BI platforms and practices to Microsoft Fabric, including data modeling, semantic layer design, and scalable analytics delivery.  

3\. Establishes and operationalizes a centralized BI delivery model (e.g., hub\-and\-spoke) to ensure consistency, quality, and scalability of analytics solutions.  

4\. Partners with Data Governance to embed data standards, data quality practices, and data stewardship into BI development and delivery processes.  

5\. Defines, implements, and enforces BI development standards, including data contracts, data products design and UX, certification processes, and lifecycle management.  

6\. Oversees the design, development, and maintenance of dashboards, reports, and analytical data products that support executive and operational decision\-making.  

7\. Serves as a strategic advisor to business leaders, translating business needs into analytical solutions and guiding data\-driven decisions.  

8\. Leads intake, prioritization, and execution of BI initiatives, ensuring alignment with institutional priorities and resource capacity.  

9\. Supervises and develops a team of BI analysts, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement.  

10\. Transforms BI team capabilities from report developers to consultative analytics partners, emphasizing storytelling, insight generation, and business impact.  

11\. Ensures integration of BI solutions across enterprise data platforms, including data warehouse, lakehouse, and downstream applications.  

12\. Establishes performance metrics and KPIs for BI delivery, adoption, data quality, and business value realization.  

13\. Collaborates with data scientists to support advanced analytics use cases, serving as a sounding board and ensuring alignment with enterprise data architecture.  

14\. Oversees ad hoc analytics and special projects, ensuring timely and high\-quality delivery of insights to support strategic initiatives.  

15\. Implements and promotes data literacy initiatives across the institution to increase effective use of BI solutions.  

16\. Ensures compliance with University policies; regulatory requirements; and data privacy, security, and governance standards.

title: Business Intelligence Analyst - Senior
organization: Cunningham Brothers Auto Parts of Charlotte
description:
**Overview**

We are seeking a dynamic and highly skilled Senior Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst to join our innovative analytics team. In this pivotal role, you will leverage your expertise in data analysis, database management, and visualization tools to transform complex data sets into actionable insights that drive strategic decision\-making. Your energetic approach and analytical prowess will empower teams across the organization to optimize operations, enhance customer experiences, and unlock new growth opportunities. This position offers an exciting opportunity to lead high\-impact projects within a fast\-paced, collaborative environment committed to excellence and continuous improvement.

**Duties**

* Lead the design, development, and deployment of comprehensive dashboards and reports using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, and Looker to visualize key performance metrics.
* Collaborate with stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into effective data solutions that support strategic initiatives.
* Manage end\-to\-end project lifecycles utilizing Agile methodologies and adhere to SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) best practices for delivering reliable BI solutions.
* Develop and maintain ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes using Talend, Python, Bash scripts, or VBA to ensure accurate data integration from diverse sources including Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, and Linked Data repositories.
* Perform advanced data analysis using R, SQL, and analytics techniques to identify trends, patterns, and insights that inform business strategies.
* Design and optimize relational databases with a focus on database design principles to support scalable analytics environments.
* Conduct business analysis by understanding operational workflows and translating them into technical specifications for BI systems enhancement.
* Oversee project management activities related to BI initiatives, ensuring timely delivery while managing scope and resources effectively.
* Maintain comprehensive documentation of data models, workflows, and system architecture using Visio or similar tools for clarity and future reference.
* Stay current with industry best practices in analytics tools like Power BI, Looker, Tableau, and emerging technologies such as linked data integration for richer insights.

**Skills**

* Proficiency in visualization platforms including Tableau, Power BI, and Looker for creating compelling dashboards.
* Strong experience with Agile project management methodologies and SDLC processes for delivering high\-quality BI solutions on time.
* Expertise in ETL tools such as Talend; scripting languages including Python, Bash (Unix shell), VBA; and SQL for data extraction and transformation tasks.
* Deep understanding of database systems like Microsoft SQL Server and Oracle; skilled in database design principles for scalable analytics infrastructure.
* Knowledge of R programming language for statistical analysis; familiarity with linked data concepts enhances data connectivity across sources.
* Business analysis skills to interpret operational needs into technical specifications; excellent analysis skills for identifying actionable insights from complex datasets.
* Experience with Visio or similar diagramming tools for documenting system architecture; familiarity with project management tools supports effective coordination of BI projects.
* Strong analytical mindset complemented by excellent communication skills to present findings clearly across various audiences.

Join us as a Senior Business Intelligence Analyst where your expertise will shape the future of data\-driven decision\-making!

Pay: $98\.00 \- $140\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Health insurance
* Work from home

Work Location: Remote

title: Business Intelligence Analyst (contract)
description:
**Business Intelligence Analyst (Contract)**

 *Please note that this is a contract role providing services to Microsoft through external staffing partners of Allegis Global Solutions. If you are selected for this role, you will be employed by AGS and will not be an employee of Microsoft.*

 **Summary:**


We are seeking a highly skilled and analytically driven professional to join a dynamic team focused on supporting procurement and supplier management for operating data centers. This role is central to standing up metrics reporting infrastructure by extracting data from emerging tools and transforming it into meaningful, actionable insights through dashboards and reports. The ideal candidate brings deep hands\-on expertise in business intelligence, data modeling, and data engineering, and thrives in a fast\-paced environment where technical precision and cross\-functional collaboration are essential. This is a remote, full\-time engagement offering the opportunity to make a measurable impact on regional data center operations through the development of robust reporting solutions using Microsoft's suite of analytics tools.

 **Job Responsibilities:**

* Develop, maintain, and enhance BI and analytics reports and dashboards using Power BI and related Microsoft tools
* Pull and integrate data from new and existing tools to support metrics reporting initiatives
* Work with key stakeholders across business functions to align technology and reporting solutions with business strategies
* Gather requirements from business units and translate them into technical specifications for implementation
* Support one or more highly complex business processes through data\-driven insights and reporting
* Prepare cost\-benefit and return\-on\-investment analyses to support system implementation decisions
* Demonstrate informed knowledge of business functions to resolve problems and identify improvement opportunities
* Serve as a liaison between the business community and technical teams to deliver solutions that meet user needs
* Work on multiple projects simultaneously as an active and contributing team member

 **Requirements:**

* 10 or more years of hands\-on experience in Power BI and data visualization development
* 10 or more years of experience in data modeling and data engineering
* 10 or more years of experience in advanced analytics and generating business insights
* Demonstrated experience working with Microsoft suite reporting tools in a professional setting
* Proven track record of building and maintaining dashboards and reports in enterprise environments
* Experience supporting procurement, supplier management, or data center operations is a plus
* Bachelor's degree in data analytics, information systems, computer science, computer engineering, or a related technical field required
* MBA or other related advanced degree preferred
* Power BI and data visualization development with advanced proficiency
* Data modeling and data engineering with the ability to design and manage complex data structures
* Advanced analytics and business insights generation
* Hands\-on experience with Microsoft suite reporting tools including Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, and Power Automate
* Basic knowledge of programming languages sufficient to read and understand code and basic functions
* Ability to create business solutions that increase competitive advantage
* Strong judgment in selecting methods and techniques for arriving at effective solutions
* Ability to solve complex technical problems and understand intricate process details
* High level of interpersonal and verbal communication skills to engage stakeholders at varying levels of technical knowledge
* Ability to analyze complex situations using multiple sources of information to develop innovative solutions
* Capacity to increase operating efficiency while producing high\-quality technical outputs

 **Additional Details:**

* Location: Remote
* Duration: 6 months
* Pay Range\*: $64\.50 \- $73

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
**Overview**


The Senior Data Analyst supports the Veterans Health Administration Specialty Care Program Office by providing enterprise data management, reporting, analytics, business intelligence, and automation services supporting specialty care programs, healthcare transformation initiatives, Oracle Health modernization efforts, PACT Act implementation, budget management, and organizational performance improvement activities. This position develops data\-driven solutions that support executive decision\-making across national healthcare programs.


The work is fast\-paced and highly collaborative, requiring strong clinical informatics expertise, adaptability, attention to detail, and the ability to perform effectively under pressure during critical implementation and stabilization periods.

**Responsibilities:**

* Develop, test, implement, maintain, and enhance databases, dashboards, scorecards, reports, and performance measurement tools.
* Gather, validate, reconcile, and analyze data from multiple VA and VHA data sources.
* Design and develop Power BI dashboards and executive\-level reporting solutions.
* Build automated data acquisition and reporting processes using Microsoft Power Platform technologies.
* Support integration of contractor\-developed solutions with authorized VA systems and enterprise data repositories.
* Develop data visualizations, dashboards, trend analyses, forecasting models, and operational assessments supporting healthcare and business operations.
* Convert legacy Excel\-based business processes into modern Microsoft 365 and Power Platform solutions.
* Create administrator guides, end\-user documentation, training materials, and knowledge transfer artifacts.
* Present findings and recommendations to program managers, executives, and stakeholders.
* Support performance measurement, program evaluation, strategic planning, and enterprise reporting activities.
* Analyze healthcare, operational, and financial data to identify trends, risks, gaps, and opportunities for process improvement.
* Support development of budget, workload, resource allocation, and utilization analyses for leadership.

**Qualifications:**

* Minimum 8 years of progressive data analytics experience.
* Bachelor's Degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Healthcare Informatics, Health Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Analytics, or related field. Master's preferred.
* Strong proficiency with Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse, SharePoint, and Excel.
* Experience supporting VA, VHA, federal healthcare, or Federal healthcare delivery organizations.
* Working knowledge of VHA data repositories including:
	+ Corporate Data Warehouse (CDW)
	+ VistA
	+ VSSC
	+ RAMP
	+ MedSAS/PTF
	+ MCA
	+ PCMM
	+ NPCD
	+ VETSNET
* Experience designing executive dashboards and operational performance reporting.
* Strong knowledge of data governance, analytics methodologies, and business intelligence practices.
* Experience supporting advanced analytics, automation, and emerging data\-driven technologies preferred
* Ability to communicate complex findings to technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* U.S. Citizenship required. Must be eligible to obtain a Federal security clearance/NACI background investigation.

**All candidates must be able to:**


* Sit, stand, walk, lift, squat, bend, twist, and reach above shoulders during the work shift
* Lift up to 50 lbs from floor to waist
* Lift up to 20 lbs
* Carry up to 40 lbs a reasonable distance
* Push/pull with 30 lbs of force


**All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.**

title: Team Lead, Business Intelligence
description:
**Harbor** is seeking a highly skilled and motivated **Team Lead – Business Intelligence** to join our Financial Systems consulting team. This role combines people leadership with hands\-on delivery, managing a team of BI Developers while actively contributing to client\-facing engagements. The Team Lead will play a critical role in delivering high\-quality financial reporting, analytics, and data\-driven insights for our law firm clients. This individual will ensure strong alignment between client needs, internal standards, and technical best practices, while fostering a collaborative and high\-performance team environment.



Harbor's team of business and technology experts provides professional services firms with strategic consulting, software expertise, and data\-driven solutions to enhance performance. We enable our clients to leverage their systems and data more effectively, helping them operate efficiently, strengthen client relationships, and improve profitability. This is a full\-time, fully remote position that can be based anywhere in the United States, United Kingdom or Canada.



The Team Lead – Business Intelligence is responsible for leading a team of four BI Developers, balancing approximately 20% leadership responsibilities with 80% hands\-on delivery and client utilization. The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in law firm financial systems, reporting frameworks, and BI tools, along with proven leadership experience. This role requires a strong combination of technical capability, client engagement skills, and team management.

**Team Leadership \& Management**


* Lead, mentor, and develop a team of four Business Intelligence Developers
* Assign, prioritize, and monitor workloads to ensure timely delivery of high\-quality outputs

* Foster a collaborative, accountable, and high\-performing team culture

* Act as a hands\-on contributor across BI projects, including report development, dashboard creation, semantic/tabular model design, and DAX measure development
* Partner with clients and internal stakeholders to gather, refine, and translate business requirements into technical solutions
* Deliver high\-quality reporting and analytics solutions tailored to law firm operational needs
* Ensure adherence to BI development standards, data governance practices, and documentation requirements


**Financial Reporting \& Analytics**


* Design and develop reporting solutions for key law firm financial metrics, including:
* Revenue, profitability, and realization
* Billing, collections, and Work in Progress (WIP)
* Timekeeper performance and utilization
* Translate complex financial and operational data into clear, actionable, well\-designed reports and dashboards
* Improve reporting efficiency through automation and optimized data models


**Technical Oversight**


* Provide guidance on BI tools and technologies (e.g., Power BI, Fabric, SQL, data lakes)
* Ensure data integrity, accuracy, and consistency across all reporting solutions
* Review and validate deliverables produced by team members
* Support best practices in semantic/tabular modeling, DAX, performance tuning, and solution design


**Process Improvement \& Innovation**


* Identify opportunities to enhance reporting frameworks and analytics capabilities
* Drive continuous improvement initiatives within the BI function
* Stay current with emerging BI tools, trends, and law firm industry practices

#### **Education, Experience, and Other Requirements:**


* 7\+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field
* 2\+ years of experience leading or managing technical teams
* Proven experience delivering financial reporting solutions, ideally within law firms or professional services

title: Business Intelligence Analyst
description:
THE ORGANIZATION
Entrepreneurs’ Organization (“EO”) is a global nonprofit membership organization and peer network for entrepreneurs, connecting approximately 20,000 members through more than 220 chapters worldwide. EO’s purpose is to move the world forward by unlocking the full potential of entrepreneurs. Since 1987, EO has helped entrepreneurs achieve transformational growth through life\-enhancing connections, shared experiences, and collaborative learning. Guided by EO’s core values, trust and respect, thirst for learning, think big, be bold, and together we grow, EO’s international staff works across diverse cultures in support of EO’s global community. As an EO employee, you will work in a global entrepreneurial community and have opportunities for learning, collaboration, and professional growth. EO offers a competitive total rewards package, including benefits and professional development support, and a flexible work environment, subject to role, location, and applicable plan terms.  

POSITION SUMMARY
The Business Intelligence Analyst builds and maintains dashboards and reports that help EO leaders and teams understand performance and take action. EO is a global organization. Audiences range from the Global Board to regional staff and volunteer chapter officers, with different experience using data. Clear, accurate reporting is essential so insights are used, not ignored.  

You will develop executive dashboards, operational reports, and self\-service views where appropriate. You will also support teams across EO (chapter operations, member engagement, growth, product teams, and others) that maintain their own dashboards and reports in BI tools, spreadsheets, or custom applications. You help them use consistent, approved data sources when possible so metrics are not rebuilt differently in every tool. You help document major reporting in use: what exists, who maintains it, and what data it relies on. You are not expected to own every team\-built report or every technical integration.  

You will work closely with other analysts and with EO’s technology team to ensure reporting is accurate and sustainable. When AI features are available in the BI platform, you will use them with the same standards for accuracy and review as manually built reports. Additional priorities such as expanded self\-service or broader regional support will depend on business needs and team capacity.  

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES**Central Dashboard and report development*** Design, build, and maintain dashboards and operational reports on EO’s BI platform.
* Present data clearly for varied audiences, from executives to regional and chapter\-facing staff.
* Apply consistent visual standards and report structures for enterprise reporting.
* Support recurring reporting cycles (monthly, quarterly, annual) in line with leadership priorities and standard business definitions.
* Gather requirements and feedback from stakeholders and refine reporting over time.

**Support for decentralized reporting*** Work with chapter operations, member engagement, product teams, and other groups on dashboards and reports they maintain outside central BI.
* Help teams connect local reporting to core or approved data sources and templates when available.
* Identify when a team\-owned report should be checked against standard definitions and involve analytics or technology colleagues as needed.
* Direct teams to standard enterprise or shared reports when those meet the business need, to limit unnecessary duplication.
* Contribute reporting and data\-display requirements when teams evaluate or change tools or workflows.

**Reporting inventory and documentation*** Maintain an inventory of significant reporting assets: purpose, owner, audience, primary data sources, tool or location, and refresh frequency. Update when major reports are launched or retired.
* Document sources, report logic, and known limitations for official reporting and for supported team\-owne

title: Data Analyst, Energy Markets
description:
The Data Analyst supports the sale and tracking of energy and environmental commodities produced by anaerobic digestion facilities, including renewable natural gas (RNG), electricity, and carbon credits (e.g., CFR, RINs, LCFS, RECs). This role is focused on data management, contract support, and market research. The Analyst will work closely with the operations, finance, policy, and business development teams to ensure accurate reporting and help optimize revenue. This position offers strong exposure to renewable energy and carbon markets, environmental analysis tools, and commercial strategy in a growing company.

**Duties \& Responsibilities** 

* Support administration of energy offtake agreements, including tracking, pricing and delivery schedules
* Help prepare documentation for verification, audits, and reporting
* Ensure data accuracy and completeness for compliance purposes; compile market data and summarize trends for internal stakeholders
* Maintain organized records of contracts, amendments, and counterparties
* Track production volumes, energy sales, and environmental credit generation
* Maintain spreadsheets and internal systems to ensure accurate and up\-to\-date data
* Prepare regular reports on production, sales, pricing, and revenue
* Monitor carbon credit markets (CFR, RINS, LCFs, RECs)
* Support pricing analysis and benchmarking efforts
* Work with operations teams to confirm production and delivery data
* Support finance with revenue tracking and reconciliation
* Assist commercial team with ad hoc analysis and deal support
* Support policy team by developing one\-pagers and informing outlook on incoming policies/programs
* Perform other job\-related duties as assigned

**Education/Licenses/Certifications** 

* Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science, Business, Finance, Economics, Data Science or related field

**Knowledge \& Experience** 

* Two (2\) or more years’ experience of related work experience, or master’s degree
* Experience in renewable energy, circular economy, sustainability or commodity markets a plus

**Skills \& Abilities** 

* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Strong analytical and quantitative skills (comfort with math, statistics, and building models to support forecasting and decision\-making)
* Highly skilled with Microsoft Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).
* Experience with PowerBI preferred
* Excellent attention to detail and accuracy
* Proactively establish trust and credibility with coworkers by maintaining accountability
* Contribute to the team dynamic by promoting inclusion, support, and collaboration amongst coworkers
* Strong organizational skills and ability to effectively manage time across competing priorities
* Ability to be a team\-player for overall company success

**Work Environment** 

Remote, with 15% travel required

**Physical Requirements** 

The physical demands described here are representative of those which must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of the job.

* Prolonged period of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

*Generate Upcycle is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate against otherwise qualified applicants on the basis of actual or perceived race, color, creed, religion, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or handicap, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.*

title: Business Intelligence Developer
description:
.


### **About Tarpon Health, Inc.**


Tarpon Health is a healthcare automation company that helps providers optimize their operations through technology. We bring together healthcare providers with like\-minded organizations to accelerate automation in the revenue cycle. Our community of experts and peers helps teams overcome expertise and capacity constraints to build automations that enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve patient experience.
### **Description**


**Title: Business Intelligence Developer**

**Department:** AI Solutions

**Reports to:** Director of AI Strategy \& Solutions

**Location:** Remote

 **Summary:**

As a Business Intelligence Developer at Tarpon Health, you will build the statistical models and data infrastructure that power our AI solutions. You will work with healthcare operations and revenue cycle data, turning raw and often messy datasets into clean, reliable inputs that our AI and machine learning systems can act on. Your work sits at the intersection of data science and applied AI, and it directly shapes the quality of the solutions we deliver to clients. This role suits analytical builders who are comfortable writing code, thinking carefully about data quality and methodology, and communicating findings to both technical and business audiences. You will work closely with software engineers, data scientists, and delivery teams to make sure the models and metrics you build hold up in production and in practice.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

* Design and build statistical models that feed AI and machine learning solutions for healthcare operations and revenue cycle use cases.
* Write optimized queries and scripts in Python and SQL to extract, transform, and load data across complex datasets and systems.
* Collaborate with engineers and data scientists to integrate models and data pipelines into the solutions we deliver to clients.
* Define and document metrics, data definitions, and methodology so the team can build on your work with confidence.
* Evaluate the accuracy and reliability of existing data assets and recommend improvements where needed.
* Stay current on developments in data science, machine learning, and AI tools, and bring useful ideas back to the team.
* Use AI tools such as Claude to accelerate analysis, generate code, and improve the efficiency of your workflow.

 **Required Qualifications:**

* 3\+ years of experience in analytics, data science, or a closely related role.
* Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
* Experience building statistical models and applying core machine learning concepts in a business or production context.
* Hands\-on experience with data visualization and business intelligence tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar.
* Strong understanding of data structures, data modeling, and data quality principles.
* Proficiency in Python for data analysis, scripting, and model development.

 **Preferred Qualifications:**

* Background in data science, including formal training or applied project work in machine learning and statistical modeling.
* Experience using AI tools such as Claude to support analysis and development work.
* Familiarity with healthcare, revenue cycle, or EMR environments (for example, Epic, Cerner, or Athenahealth).
* Experience with cloud data platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
* Familiarity with version control using Git and collaborative development workflows.

 **Note:** This job description is not intended to be all\-inclusive. Additional duties and responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to meet the needs of the organization.

  

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, merit, and busin

title: Business Intelligence Lead, Product Analytics
description:
**Become a part of our caring community**
=========================================


The Business Intelligence Lead is an individual contributor role embedded into a digital product team. This is not a generalist analytics role but requires someone who has spent their career in product analytics, understands how digital product teams work, and can operate as a peer to senior product managers and designers rather than a support function beneath them. The right candidate has lived inside a product organization, shaped roadmap decisions with data, and knows the difference between reporting on what has happened and explaining why it matters.
Humana has articulated a strategic vision to drive the digital adoption and a digital\-first strategy. The Consumer Digital Business Intelligence team provides support by partnering with digital product owners and enterprise partners in understanding not only the digital footprint, but the upstream and downstream impact of digital behavior. We are dedicated to improving the experience of our members through delivering analytics and insights.


The Business Intelligence Lead solves complex business problems and issues using a variety of internal Humana data and tools. The Business Intelligence Lead works closely with product leaders, engineers, and designers to deliver insights related to digital activity, efficiency, emerging trends, process improvement, pilot measurement, and member, provider and associate experience. As a Business Intelligence Lead, you will:

* Serve as the primary analytics partner to your assigned product team. Attending sprint reviews, PI planning, and discovery sessions as a full participant, not a data supplier
* Own the measurement plan for your assigned product portfolio. Defining KPIs, funnels, and success metrics before they ship
* Be the first touchpoint for product leader data needs, responsible for identifying solutions both home\-grown and in partnership with other data and analytic teams, including our own data science team.
* Evaluate the effectiveness of the digital product strategy and recommend adjustments when needed to drive outcomes.
* Build and maintain product performance dashboards in PowerBI that drive active product decisions
* Analyze member behavior across the digital experience, funnel drop\-off, feature adoption, session depth, and engagement patterns
* Work with Data Science teams to design and analyze A/B tests and experiments, communicate results clearly without overstating significance
* Write and maintain SQL and Python to support analysis and automate recurring data pulls
* Translate complex findings into clear, opinionated recommendations for product mangers and senior leadership
* May require 5% travel if outside of Louisville, KY

**Use your skills to make an impact**
=====================================

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor's Degree and 6\+ years of experience in product analytics, OR Master's degree and 4\+ years of experience in product analytics OR 8\+ years of hands\-on experience in product analytics without a degree
* Prior experience embedded within a consumer\-facing digital product organization
* Proven ability to influence product roadmap and prioritization decisions with data
* Strong SQL, Advanced PowerBI, and basic Python
* Proven experience with product funnel and behavioral event data
* A/B testing experience, test design, statistical interpretation and clear stakeholder communication
* Deep familiarity with the product development lifecycle and where analytics fits in each stage

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in consumer health, digital health, or member\-facing health plan applications
* Advanced degree in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Economics, Finance, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering or related field


Travel: While this is a remote position, occasional travel to Humana's offices for training or meetings may be required.**Scheduled Weekly Hours**

title: Senior QA Automation Engineer
description:
**Job Description**

Senior QA Automation Engineer to manage the testing of our APIs using ONB enterprise automation testing frameworks and testing architecture. This role blends traditional QA manual testing responsibilities with advanced automation strategy using Playwright. The ideal candidate will have strong programming knowledge, hands\-on experience in test automation, expert level knowledge in modern test tools and a strong ability to complete deliverables on time.

**Core QA Engineer Responsibilities:**

* Participate in project kickoff, lead automation scoping, lead automation project estimation and represent Automation team in ongoing project meetings.
* Design, develop, and execute automated test cases for ONB APIs and report test results to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders based on business and technical requirements.
* Collaborate with developers, business analysts, product managers, and all QA memebers to ensure high\-quality software releases.
* Analyze test results, identify defects, and track them through resolution.
* Participate in sprint planning, daily stand\-ups, and retrospectives as part of an Agile development team.
* Maintain and improve QA logical and physical architecture, test tools, and QA automation processes.

**Automation Responsibilities:** 

o Create , maintain and Peer Review code and help improving the code to ensure timely delivery of automation projects.

o Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within the automation team.

* Automation Pipeline Development \& Maintenance:

o Design, implement, and maintain CI/CD\-integrated automation pipelines using CI tool and Git Repositories.

o Ensure scalability, reliability, and performance of automated test execution across all environments.

o Monitor pipeline health and troubleshoot failures proactively.

* Full\-Stack Test Development (API, UI, 3rd party Integration, Database, Visual) using Playwright and Typescript

o Hands\-On AI/ML Test and Tool Integration into Framework using GitHub Copilot, MCP Agents, LLMs.

**Qualifications and Education Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from a reputed University.
* 10\+ years of experience in software QA including 5 in a senior role.
* 7\+ years in test automation including 4 in a senior role.

Pay: Up to $120,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

title: Scrum Master - Project Delivery Management Engineer II
description:
Are you an experienced, passionate pioneer in technology who wants to work in a collaborative environment? As an experienced Scrum Master \- Project \- Delivery Management Engineer , you will have the ability to share new ideas and collaborate on projects as a consultant without the extensive demands of travel. If so, consider an opportunity with Deloitte under our Project Delivery Talent Model. Project Delivery Model (PDM) is a talent model that is tailored specifically for long\-term, onsite client service delivery.  

  

Recruiting for this role ends on July 8 th , 2026  

  

**Work you'll do/Responsibilities**  

  

You will serve as a **Scrum Master** for a **Data \& Analytics** agile product team operating in a **SAFe** environment as part of a **Data \& Analytics Foundry** staff augmentation program supporting numerous business product teams (approx. **235 resources across onshore and offshore**).  

  

* Facilitate SAFe/Agile team ceremonies and enable consistent iteration execution across the product team.

  

* Partner with the Product Owner and team to support backlog readiness and day\-to\-day flow of work through the sprint/iteration.

  

* Coordinate with onshore/offshore team members and adjacent product teams to manage dependencies and maintain delivery alignment.

  

* Identify and remove impediments; manage and escalate risks/issues to keep delivery on track.

  

* Track and communicate delivery status and team progress; drive continuous improvement actions.

* Independently and collaboratively lead client engagement workstreams focused on improvement, optimization, and transformation of processes including implementing leading practice workflows, addressing deficits in quality, and driving operational outcomes.

  

**The Team**  

  

AI \& Engineering leverages cutting\-edge engineering capabilities to build, deploy, and operate integrated/verticalized sector solutions in software, data, AI, network, and hybrid cloud infrastructure. These solutions are powered by engineering for business advantage, transforming mission\-critical operations. We enable clients to stay ahead with the latest advancements by transforming engineering teams and modernizing technology \& data platforms. Our delivery models are tailored to meet each client's unique requirements.  

  

**Qualifications**  

  

**Required**  

* 4\+ years of experience supporting Agile delivery as a Scrum Master
* 4\+ years of experience facilitating Agile ceremonies (iteration/sprint planning, daily standups, reviews/demos, retrospectives) and driving impediment removal in an Agile environment.
* 4\+ years of experience with project management for AI and data/analytics initiatives
* Bachelor's degree, preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer Engineering, or related IT discipline; or equivalent experience
* Limited immigration sponsorship may be available.
* Ability to travel 10%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve.

  

**Preferred**  

* SAFe certification and/or hands\-on SAFe experience
* Analytical ability to manage multiple projects and prioritize tasks into manageable work products.
Excellent written and communication skills

title: Technical Project Manager / Scrum Master
description:
**Overview**
------------

BerryDunn’s **Healthcare Analytics Practice Group (HAPG), Data Management and Strategy (DMS)** team is seeking a **Technical Project Manager / Scrum Master** to lead and deliver complex data management and system technology initiatives. This role requires a blend of strong project management leadership, technical expertise, and Agile delivery capabilities to successfully manage multiple concurrent projects in a hybrid Agile environment.  

The ideal candidate brings deep experience leading technical teams, strong subject\-matter expertise in healthcare data and systems, and the ability to operate at the intersection of project management, business analysis, and data engineering collaboration. **Location:** This position can be remote within the United States or based in one of BerryDunn’s office locations. **Travel Expectations:** Up to 25% travel may be required depending on client needs and project demands.
**You Will**
------------

* Engage in discussions with the team, clients, and project sponsors to define project objectives, success criteria, and priorities
* Lead and deliver multiple concurrent projects using a hybrid Agile (Scrum) and traditional project management approach
* Develop and maintain project plans, schedules, status reports, and delivery roadmaps
* Manage project risks, issues, dependencies, and resources across projects
* Facilitate Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand\-ups, backlog refinement, retrospectives, and demos
* Provide technical oversight across the full Systems Development Lifecycle (SDLC) for assigned projects
* Collaborate with data engineers on data pipelines, warehousing, reporting, and integration solutions
* Elicit, document, and manage business and system requirements
* Translate business needs into clear functional and technical specifications
* Serve as a liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams
* Perform and oversee SQL\-based data analysis, validation, and quality assurance activities
* Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and team members
* Provide clear, consistent communication and deliver presentations
* Use Artificial Intelligence tools to enhance productivity and improve project deliverables
* Provide guidance and mentorship to junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement
* Keep abreast of the latest trends and technologies in data engineering, healthcare analytics, data science, and AI to ensure BerryDunn remains at the forefront of the industry
* Ensure all data solutions comply with industry standards, regulations, and security protocols, particularly those specific to healthcare



**You Have**
------------

* Minimum of 5 years of experience leading enterprise data management, data integration, or data analytics technical projects
* Experience managing projects in Agile/Scrum or hybrid delivery environments
* Strong background in system implementation, data engineering, or analytics projects
* Experience with business analysis, requirements gathering, and requirements management
* Working knowledge of SQL for data analysis
* Experience developing, maintaining, and managing project artifacts
* Hands\-on experience with managing/administering projects in Jira
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, organizational, and leadership skills
* Ability to manage multiple priorities
* A team\-oriented mindset with a friendly and professional demeanor, coupled with the ability to remain flexible amid changing needs and priorities
* Ability to work effectively with coworkers and diverse clients through periods of both intense interaction and self\-directed work
* A bachelor's degree in a related field; Advanced degrees or relevant certifications are a plus

title: Project Manager
description:
The Program Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, and driving the successful execution of deliverables across Cascadience, including digital product development, launch readiness, marketing initiatives, operational projects, vendor management, and organizational growth efforts.

This position is assignment focused on supporting the successful launch and operationalization of Volio, an AI\-powered mobile application designed to support neurodivergent students. Following launch, the role is expected to expand into broader program management responsibilities across Cascadience's portfolio of products, services, partnerships, and strategic initiatives.

**SUMMARY OF JOB\-SPECIFIC DUTIES \& REQUIREMENTS**

Program \& Execution Management

* Own and coordinate multiple concurrent projects across product, marketing, operations, and partnerships
* Maintain integrated program plans, timelines, milestones, dependencies, and risks
* Ensure delivery against priorities, scope, and deadlines across all workstreams
* Identify risks early.

Product \& Vendor Coordination

* Serve as primary liaison between Cascadience and external development vendors
* Ensure alignment between business goals and technical execution
* Drive clarity on requirements, decisions, and dependencies

Marketing \& Launch Execution

Process \& Policies

* Support execution of operational improvements, systems, and organizational initiatives

Stakeholder \& Executive Communication

* Provide regular updates to leadership on status, risks, and priorities

· Represent Viability positively and accurately internally and externally. Uphold Viability’s best interests.

· Uphold dignity and inclusion for all. Work as a partner and collaborator within a team environment.

· Attend all required meetings and initiate meetings as needed.

· Other duties as assigned

* Demonstrated ability to drive execution without direct authority
* High reliability, accountability, and follow\-through

title: Senior Agile Lead
description:
Working for a company like Smile Digital Health means supporting our mandate for ***\#BetterGlobalHealth***. We strive towards this goal every day, and the results can be seen in the impact of our innovative health data platform and data management solutions, which are used in over 20 countries. We were \#19 on Deloitte's Technology Fast 50 Ranking for 2024!

Smile Digital Health makes it easy for healthcare stakeholders to collect and exchange data with our leading FHIR\-based data liberation platform.

At its heart, the Smile platform enables people and organizations to better manage healthcare data. We help generate and liberate structured healthcare data to ensure effective delivery across care teams and health systems bringing ***\#BetterGlobalHealth*** to patients everyday!  

  

**Apply today and find plenty of reasons to SMILE!**  

The Agile Lead is a pivotal role in the successful development, delivery, and implementation of Smile’s healthcare interoperability platform to customers. As a strategic facilitator, systems thinker, and change agent in this role, the Agile Lead will have a deep understanding of agile and lean methodologies, practices and frameworks that will enable teams to deliver high\-quality solutions efficiently and responsively.
### **Primary Functions**

* Collaborate on the evolution of the product delivery framework which supports repeatable and scalable delivery patterns.

* Guide teams in capacity\-based planning, using tools and methods that support sustainable delivery.
* Facilitate the removal of impediments and blockers proactively to maintain project momentum and productivity.
* Educate and coach teams and the broader organization on agile mindsets, practices, and frameworks through workshops and training sessions.
* Accountable for ensuring that all working hours are accurately reported in Netsuite on a daily or weekly basis, that the majority of (if not all) hours are tracked as billable and that the project management tool in Netsuite is properly and fully utilized.
* Tracking and reporting of billable hours is a critical aspect of project management and delivery to our customers and this is a major area of accountability.
* Comply with the privacy, security and confidentiality policies.


### **Prerequisites**

* 7\+ years of experience in Agile delivery, with a focus on driving value in technical or software implementation environments
* Active agile practitioner certification (CSM, A\-CSM, PSM, etc.)
* Experience facilitating development and delivery involving cloud infrastructure builds, platform implementations, and custom software development.
* In\-depth understanding of modern cloud infrastructure concepts (AWS, Azure, Kubernetes) and familiarity with software deployment pipelines (CI/CD)
* Demonstrated ability to coordinate cross\-functional technical teams in complex implementation scenarios.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills and ability to engage effectively with technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* Proven ability to guide cross\-functional teams in delivering high\-impact solutions in dynamic, customer\-facing environments.
* Healthcare technology experience or familiarity with healthcare interoperability (FHIR standards, clinical integration, EHRs), pr

This position will require continuous collaboration and consultation with leaders, contributors, and various level stakeholders to ensure the desired business outcomes are implemented. In addition to this, the scrum master should be well versed in executing work utilizing the Agile methodology. This includes facilitation of scrum ceremonies (daily stand\-up, sprint planning \& backlog grooming meetings). multiple processes, systems, functions, services, and/or products. • Fosters information sharing, creating a collaborative environment • Allocates and monitors resource allocations and forecasting for team members on the scrum team. • Cohesive project facilitation, planning, collaboration, analysis, and guidance, ensuring that solutions achieve the expected business benefit or result. • Active participation in all phases technology delivery: which may include discovery, assessment, initiation, planning, design, development, testing, execution, deployment, post implementation support etc. • manage, escalate, and mitigate all issues and risks, engaging required parties, and escalating as needed, to drive to resolution and project deliverables remain on track. • Proactively evaluate project activities, build contingency plans, execute corrective action, and monitor issue resolution to mitigate risks such as scope and/or variances in desired results. • Track activity progress and build communication plan for implementation to ensure all impacted parties (up\-stream/down\-stream) are informed of next steps for completion and project status. • Effectively manage team/stakeholder expectations and engagement through clear and frequent communication regarding status updates, critical path progress, or scrum of scrums meetings. • Sets the tone in shaping the team’s culture, fostering an environment for high\-performing team dynamics, continuous flow \& improvements Required Qualifications • 4\-7 years related experience in project management or scrum master role • Understanding of Agile at scale philosophies \& methodologies. • Experience working in an Agile organization using Scrum or Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) • Strong technical aptitude and experience leading teams in delivering quality software • Experience includes development and management of multiple projects simultaneously • Proactive, problem\-solving approach – someone who offers solutions not problems for others to sol

title: Scrum Master w/EDI Experience
description:
Overview:

TISTA is seeking a **Scrum Master** in support of a critical application within the federal agency. The Scrum Master will lead a scrum team, provide management, steer direction, administration, quality assurance, and leadership of staff execution for assigned projects. As our Scrum Master you will provide day\-to\-day support and management of scrum teams including quality monitoring and SME\-level inputs to improve the overall performance of the assigned projects; support of configuration and release management, and assessment and authorization to VA IT development/ sustainment project(s) as needed.  

At TISTA, you’ll do meaningful, mission‑driven work that improves lives alongside teammates you trust and leaders who are transparent and supportive. We invest in your learning and internal mobility so you can build a career that keeps advancing. We’re proud to serve and hire Veterans, and we put people first in everything we do. **TISTA** associates enjoy above Industry Healthcare Benefits, Remote Working Options, Paid Time Off, Training/Certification opportunities, Healthcare Savings Account \& Flexible Savings Account, Paid Life Insurance, Short\-term \& Long\-term Disability, 401K Match, Tuition Reimbursement, Employee Assistance Program, Paid Holidays, Military Leave, and much more!
Responsibilities:
* Provide day\-to\-day management guidance and direction to scrum teams in the execution of tasks.
* Manage resources and project based on timelines established in the project plan, and monitor progress.
* Use proven software development methodologies, including Large Scale Scrum (LeSS), SAFe, VIP and Process Asset Library (PAL) that assure that all contract\-driven activities are identified, documented, and tracked so that the contract shall continuously be evaluated and monitored for timely and quality service.
* Act as primary interface (liaison) with client and ensure client's needs are met within the scope of this project and our contract.
* Provide project financial and resource management.
* Plan and conduct Program Increment (PI) Planning, manage PI\-Planning session in accordance with SAFe and in alignment with VA VIP process.
* Provide detailed metrics and dashboard covering tasks, assigned resources, and story points including burn down charts.
* Identify, communicate, mitigate and manage project risks; provide weekly updates to manager and PMSS Risk Manager on issues/risks requiring escalation.
* Plan and support Sprint and Build releases for all environment, and coordinate with Test and CM Manager to ensure adequate and timely support.
* Collect and maintain all release documents and ensure that compliance user stories have been completed and approved by assigned Release Manager.
* Ensure all Scrum ceremonies (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Backlog Refinement, Sprint Review, Retrospective) happen efficiently and are productive.
* Ensure dependencies are surfaced, communicated, tracked, and resolved efficiently leading to fewer delays and smoother collaboration.
* Drives retrospective meetings to identify actionable improvements.
* Helps the team set achievable goals and facilitates discussions.
* Fosters an environment of open communication, trust, and teamwork.
* Collaborate with the Business Analyst and Program Manager to deliver weekly, iteration\-based, and quarterly reports detailing accomplishments, objectives etc
* Assists with effective backlog management. Helps clarify goals, scope, and product domain.

  


Qualifications:
* 10 years of experience supporting software development programs.
* 5 years of experience leading a program using either LeSS or SAFe (required).
* Strong experience as a Scrum Master in an Agile development methodology.
* Proven ability to lead scrum teams in the execution of project tasks and providing monitoring including risk/issues management.
* Experience managing scrum teams within the VA, specifically working knowledge of VA's VIP management process (nice to have).
*

title: Asst. Store Manager - Boca Raton, FL
description:
We Live to Discover
  

  

Jack Wolfskin lives to discover and encourages people to go on new outdoor adventures, connecting with nature and rewilding themselves. Through sustainable German engineered apparel, footwear and equipment that protects and inspires, we enable everyone to experience more moments of outdoor discovery. Our success is based on a shared wanderlust and the collective input from our team of free spirits, forward thinkers and co\-discoverers. Sharing the same values we focus on having a positive impact through improved product innovations, more sustainable solutions and business practices as well as advocacy programs.
  

We encourage an open forum of ideas throughout all departments and honor collaborative discoverers who lead by introducing new paths. A career with Jack Wolfskin is anything but ordinary.


The Assistant Store Manager is responsible for supporting the day\-to\-day retail operations of his / her applicable store inclusive of sales and service, team leadership and operational excellence.


**ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Provide an approachable and efficient sales and service focus on the floor to achieve sales goals and deliver exceptional service
* Connect with in\-store clients and develop / retain a following of repeat clients to drive sales
* Assist in developing and implementing strategies to drive sales and key performance indicators

* Manage and maintain store cleanliness and organization, including front of house and stock space

* Ability to recruit, train, manage and develop a team

* Ability to work non\-traditional hours; weekends, evenings and holidays

* Walk, sit, stand, bend, reach and move continually during working hours

title: Assistant Store Manager
description:
**Join Our Team at Grandpa Joe's Candy Shop!**


Are you ready to be part of something big? At Grandpa Joe's Candy Shop, we're continuing to grow and are excited to expand our team. Currently, we have an immediate opening for an experienced Assistant Store Manager who is passionate about crafting extraordinary experiences for our customers.

**About Us:**


Grandpa Joe's Candy Shop was born in Pittsburgh, PA, in 2012\. We've grown from a single store to 23 locations (and counting!) across Pennsylvania, Ohio, Florida, West Virginia,Texas and Minnesota. We also offer national distribution of candy and soda to over 15,000 independent retailers.

**The Role:**


This role is tailor\-made for seasoned managers who have thrived in larger retail environments but are now seeking greater autonomy in shaping day\-to\-day operations. If you're a master of team building, possess a keen eye for merchandising, and relish the prospect of spending most of your time engaging with customers and driving sales, this opportunity is for you.


As a Store Manager at Grandpa Joe's, you'll lead the charge of curating unforgettable experiences for our guests. From leading the team to greeting customers, your leadership with set the pace and tone of our store. Additionally, you'll play a pivotal role in merchandising our store to captivate and delight our guests, ensuring that each interaction leaves a lasting impression.

**Why Join Us:**

* **Opportunity for Influence:** Thrive in a role where your voice matters. We're seeking seasoned managers who relish the chance to shape the day\-to\-day operations and steer the business towards success.
* **Passion for People:** If you excel at team building, have an eye for merchandising, and enjoy face\-to\-face customer interactions, this role is made for you.
* **Opportunity for Growth**: We're not just expanding our business; we're growing our team. As we expand, there are opportunities for advancement and professional development. Your hard work won't go unnoticed here.
* **Make Your Mark**: At Grandpa Joe's, your work matters. Every idea, every interaction, and every effort contribute to our success. You'll have the chance to innovate, collaborate, and see the direct impact of your contributions.
* **Be Part of a Thriving Team**: Our team is supportive, collaborative, and passionate about what we do. When you join Grandpa Joe's, you're joining a community of like\-minded individuals who share your love for candy and customer service.
* **Community Involvement**: We love being part of each unique neighborhood we're located in, whether it's participating in community events or simply getting to know our neighbors. When you join Grandpa Joe's, you're not just joining a company; you're joining a community.

**What You'll Get:**

* **Competitive Benefits**: We offer competitive compensation based on experience and opportunities for bonuses and incentives. Eligible for PTO, health, dental, and vision benefits after 60 days. Team member discounts are available for all team members.
* **Training and Support**: We'll provide you with the tools and training you need to succeed. Whether you're a candy connoisseur or new to the world of sweets, we'll help you become a candy expert in no time.
* **Fun and Fulfillment**: At Grandpa Joe's, work isn't just a job; it's an adventure. We like to have a sense of humor, so get ready to laugh, learn, and make memories that will last a lifetime.

**Here's What We're Looking For:**


As Assistant Store Manager, you'll wear many hats, managing various aspects of our store operations, including opening and closing procedures, cash management, team scheduling, training and coaching, inventory control, shift coverage, merchandising, stocking, and ensuring exceptional customer service. You'll set the standard by leading from the front, driving sales, and cultivating a culture of excellence.

* **Experienced**: Ideal candidates will have a proven track record in retail management withi

title: Assistant Store Manager - Edwin Watts Golf
description:
**What We Offer:**

* Our Assistant Managers receive base pay \+ quarterly bonuses
* We offer paid vacation, 401(k) matching, and health benefits with company contribution to the HSA for full\-time employees!
* There is no better time to work in the golf industry! All employees receive a strong employee discount, golf\-specific learning opportunities, and hands\-on training.
* Edwin Watts Golf is part of the Worldwide Golf family. Worldwide Golf has over 95 stores in 25\+ states. We’ve been in business over 50 years and we are growing!
* Worldwide Golf is one of the largest golf retailers in the world, giving you more opportunities for growth and mobility.

**Job Description:**


The Assistant Manager at Edwin Watts Golf is responsible for promoting and maintaining a store culture that is consistent with Worldwide Golf’s values, including the “Sure No Problem” philosophy, which has made Worldwide Golf what it is today. "Sure No Problem" means putting the customer first, and doing whatever we can to ensure a memorable and positive customer experience.


The Assistant Manager executes the day\-to\-day plans and strategies developed by the Store Manager. The Assistant Manager also has shared ownership for creating a positive golf store experience. Responsibilities Include:

* Maintain strong knowledge of all golf products and ensure that employees can educate customers on all golf products and offerings.
* Control shrink and expenses while ensuring the sales floor is adequately stocked \& signed, and golf products \& merchandise are displayed to brand presentation.
* Ensure execution of all visual merchandising programs, modules, and direction while implementing floor moves, merchandising and display maintenance.
* Ensure compliance with all policies and procedures through regular store management, meetings, store walk\-throughs and audits, etc.
* Monitor company communication tools daily and adjust schedule to execute workload as needed.
* Become a Edwin Watts Golf brand ambassador in the local community, making key external relationships and partnerships in the golf industry.
* Appropriately respond to any negative customer experience by de\-escalating the situation and making things right for the customer. Use customer feedback to recognize/coach employees.
* Help employees grow their career and golf skill set by identifying additional opportunities for growth.
* Recognize and suggest ways to improve processes.

**Qualifications Desired:**

* 2\+ years of retail management experience OR experience working in the golf industry is required.
* A strong golf background is preferred, but not required if candidate has extensive retail management experience.

**Requirements:**

* Have a working knowledge of POS register system and word\-processing, spreadsheet, inventory, internet and order processing systems.
* Ability to read, write, analyze and interpret business reports, correspondence and procedures effectively and presenting that information with excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, commissions and percentages.
* Freely access all areas of the store including sales floor, stock area and register area by standing, walking, stooping, crawling, climbing, crouching and kneeling and lifting and/or moving up to 35 pounds at any given time.
* Work varied hours/days as business dictates.


If you are looking for other job opportunities, please check out our careers page: https://careers.worldwidegolfshops.com/

**Who We Are:**


Edwin Watts Golf is part of the Worldwide Golf family, which includes well\-renowned golf retail and e\-commerce brands, including:


Roger Dunn Golf Shops \| Edwin Watts Golf \| The Golf Mart \| Golfers' Warehouse \| Van's Golf Shops \| Uinta Golf \| Las Vegas Golf Superstore \| Golfdom \| Mike's Golf Outlet \| Golf and Ski Warehouse \| Cool Clubs \| Worldwidegolf.com \| Globalgolf.com \| Fairwaystyles.com \| Getclubs.com
